steps.n: This is a helper word that makes what comes after it stronger - like adding an exclamation mark in English!
Adding آ at the end shows this is about 'we/us'. This word connects to أَعْطَيْنٰكَ (we gave) in this ayah, showing who is doing the giving.
Basic meaning of giving or presenting something to someone
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to show causing someone to do something. The و changed to ي because it's a weak letter.
Added نا at the end to show that 'we' are the ones doing the giving
Added كَ at the end to show you are receiving the giving. This word connects back to إِنَّآ (indeed we) at the start of the verse.
Root meaning relates to abundance and multiplicity
Adding و with sukun makes the word stronger, showing something very abundant or overflowing with goodness

steps.m: the great abundance (that Allah gave to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Adding 'ٱلْ' makes it refer to the specific divine abundance mentioned in the surah

steps.m: to pray or connect through worship

steps.n: The doubled ل shows emphasis or intensity in the prayer

steps.m: so pray intensely! (command to Prophet ﷺ)
steps.n: The فَ shows this is a result of what came before. This command is specifically directed to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in this context.

steps.m: to care for, develop, raise up
Lord (one who cares for and develops)
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does this action completely and perfectly
Added كَ at the end to show this belongs to you (when speaking to one male)

steps.m: to/for your Lord (connected to فَصَلِّ - so pray)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show this is who the prayer is for, connecting it to the command 'pray' (فَصَلِّ) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added اِ at start and changed ending to make it a command to one person

steps.m: and sacrifice! (command to Prophet Muhammad ﷺ)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the start to connect it with the previous command فَصَلِّ (so pray)
Adding shadda changes this from a conditional word to an emphasis word. It makes what comes after it (شَانِئَكَ - your enemy/hater) more certain and important.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Added alif after first root letter and kasra after second letter to show who does the hating
Added كَ at the end to show the hater is connected to you. This word is the subject of هُوَ (he) in this ayah.
This is a personal pronoun that refers back to شَانِئَكَ (your hater) in this ayah. It's used to emphasize who is being talked about.
Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this is a past tense verb
Changed from verb 'to cut off' to adjective meaning 'cut off/severed'

steps.m: the one who is cut off from all good (predicate of هُوَ)
steps.n: The ٱلْ makes it specific and definite. This word describes the subject هُوَ (he) in the sentence.
